pipeline.summary: Tempus launched the ArteraAI Prostate Test for metastatic patients, marking the first prostate digital pathology algorithm in the Tempus ecosystem available for clinical use.
pipeline.why_it_matters: This launch indicates new demand for lab supplies and reagents related to digital pathology and prostate cancer diagnostics, prompting engagement from lab supply sales representatives.

pipeline.summary: Tempus announced a significant expansion of new indications to its AI-enabled Next platform, including six new clinical scenarios across breast, colorectal, ovarian, prostate, and urothelial cancers, aiming to deliver real-time clinical intelligence and close critical care gaps in oncology.
pipeline.therapeutic_or_application_area: Oncology (breast, colorectal, ovarian, prostate, urothelial cancers, non-small cell lung cancer)
pipeline.why_it_matters: The expansion of the Next platform to cover more cancer types suggests increased utilization of Tempus's AI-enabled services, potentially driving demand for associated diagnostic tests and consumables.

pipeline.summary: Tempus announced a major technological upgrade to Tempus Hub, its AI-enabled smart physician platform, incorporating next-generation agentic AI architecture to streamline clinical workflows.
pipeline.why_it_matters: Platform upgrades that streamline clinical workflows can increase the efficiency and adoption of Tempus's diagnostic services, indirectly boosting demand for related lab supplies and reagents.

pipeline.summary: Tempus expanded its strategic collaboration with Bristol Myers Squibb to enhance the probability of success across clinical development programs in oncology and neuroscience.
pipeline.product_or_program: Strategic Collaboration (Oncology and Neuroscience clinical development)
pipeline.why_it_matters: Expanded collaborations with pharmaceutical companies often lead to increased research and development activities, which can drive demand for a wide range of lab supplies, reagents, and equipment.

funding.summary: Tempus AI priced an upsized offering of $460.0 million aggregate principal amount of 0.00% Convertible Senior Notes due 2032 in a private placement. The net proceeds are intended for debt repayment, capped call transactions, and general corporate purposes, which may include acquisitions or strategic investments in complementary businesses or technologies and capital expenditures.
funding.use_of_proceeds: Repayment of outstanding loans, capped call transactions, and general corporate purposes including strategic investments in complementary businesses or technologies and capital expenditures, which enable new assay development and diagnostic expansion.
funding.why_it_matters: This significant capital raise provides Tempus AI with enhanced financial flexibility to fund strategic initiatives, including the development of new diagnostic assays and expansion into new diagnostic areas, which will drive demand for lab supplies and equipment.

funding.summary: Tempus AI expanded its collaboration with Quanterix/Lucent Diagnostics on Alzheimer's disease blood tests, integrating its AI-driven platform into neurological diagnostics. This partnership is linked to Tempus's broader diagnostic ambition and increased capital access.
funding.use_of_proceeds: New assay development for Alzheimer's biomarker blood tests and broadening diagnostic reach into neurological diagnostics.
funding.why_it_matters: This collaboration signals new assay development in neurological diagnostics, which will necessitate the procurement of specialized reagents, consumables, and potentially new analytical instruments for blood-based Alzheimer's biomarker testing.

funding.summary: Tempus AI announced the clinical launch of the ArteraAI Prostate Test (mHSPC), an AI-powered prognostic test fully integrated into the Tempus platform. This test combines Tempus' genomic-based next-generation sequencing (NGS) assay results with Artera's pathology-based AI results.
funding.use_of_proceeds: New assay development, specifically an AI-powered prognostic test for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer, incorporating genomic-based NGS and pathology-based AI.
funding.why_it_matters: The launch of this new genomic and pathology-based AI test for prostate cancer will drive demand for NGS reagents, pathology consumables, and potentially new or upgraded sequencing and imaging equipment for Tempus' labs and partner facilities.

project.summary: Tempus announced a significant expansion of new indications to its AI-enabled Next platform, delivering real-time clinical intelligence across six new cancer scenarios.
project.scope: Expanded to surface insights from patients across six new clinical scenarios in breast, colorectal, NSCLC, prostate, ovarian, and urothelial cancers. A multi-center prospective study evaluated deployment across 662 eNSCLC patients at six diverse U.S. community health systems.
project.timeline: Ongoing, with a landmark study showcased at ASCO 2026.
project.why_it_matters: The expansion of this diagnostic platform to new cancer types and its deployment across multiple health systems will increase the demand for biomarker testing reagents, consumables, and potentially new diagnostic equipment.

project.summary: Tempus launched the ArteraAI Prostate Test for metastatic patients, marking the first prostate digital pathology algorithm in the Tempus ecosystem available for clinical use.
project.scope: Provides a digital pathology algorithm for prostate cancer in metastatic patients for clinical use.
project.why_it_matters: The launch of a new clinical diagnostic test will drive demand for pathology reagents, consumables, and potentially digital pathology equipment and services.

project.summary: Tempus announced a new initiative with Bristol Myers Squibb to leverage AI and multimodal real-world data to optimize clinical trial designs and enhance success rates across five initial clinical trial programs.
project.project_name: Expanded Strategic Collaboration with Bristol Myers Squibb
project.scope: Optimizing clinical trial designs and enhancing Probability of Technical & Regulatory Success (PTRS) across five initial clinical trial programs in solid tumor oncology (lung, colon, prostate cancers) and neuroscience (Alzheimer's Disease). Builds on existing Next Pathways program across 13 community-based health systems.
project.why_it_matters: This collaboration will likely increase demand for advanced genomic and molecular profiling services, associated reagents, and consumables for clinical trial support and biomarker discovery.

project.summary: Tempus entered a multi-year strategic collaboration with NYU Langone Health for a prospective observational study using serial molecular profiling, and a strategic collaboration with Blood Cancer United to develop a large real-world data registry for pediatric acute myeloid leukemia.
project.project_name: Collaborations with NYU Langone Health and Blood Cancer United
project.scope: NYU Langone Health: Prospective observational study using serial molecular profiling to track cancer evolution and treatment resistance, aiming to develop AI-powered diagnostic tools and personalized therapies. Blood Cancer United: Develop one of the largest real-world data registries for pediatric acute myeloid leukemia to accelerate research and improve treatment options.
project.why_it_matters: These collaborations will drive significant demand for molecular profiling reagents, lab consumables, and potentially specialized equipment for genomic analysis and data collection in oncology research and diagnostics.

regulatory.summary: Tempus AI, Inc. announced the clinical launch of the ArteraAI Prostate Test (mHSPC), which is CLIA-certified and CAP-accredited, integrating it into the Tempus platform.
regulatory.why_it_matters: The launch of a new CLIA-certified and CAP-accredited test indicates a need for ongoing supplies for sample processing, reagents, and quality control materials in Tempus's accredited lab.

ai_summary: Tempus is rapidly expanding its AI-driven diagnostic and platform offerings, including new prostate tests and broader cancer indications, backed by significant new funding. Strategic collaborations and technological upgrades signal increased lab activity, R&D, and potential acquisitions. This growth creates substantial opportunities for lab supply sales, requiring reagents, consumables, and instrumentation to support their expanding clinical and research operations.
